title: Data-integrity guard: auto_spec fabricated-0 FK defect -- found, repaired, and guarded
note: auto_spec was fabricating 0 into NOT-NULL FKs: 29,050 POs had no supplier, 32,744 toolings had no manufacturer, plus 29,012 PO addresses & 406 calendar rows. ALL REPAIRED & verified against the original (bffe0da3). Shipped a guard so it can't recur + a population-gap detector.

title: Nullable-FK cluster sweep
note: The auto_spec fabricated-0 guard only catches NOT-NULL columns -- nullable FKs can still silently land wrong. About to dispatch.

title: etl_core: make ETL_TARGET_SCHEMA required (code-only safety fix)
note: Stops the ETL from silently defaulting to a schema that writes into stale shells (root cause of the acme->vams schema-rename load-completeness gaps).

title: Operational fix: resolve HR punch load contention
note: Three concurrent transactions deadlock-chained inserting into the same table, stalling for 17+ minutes. Live worker resolving.

title: Verification: BOM cost drift reconciliation to the cent
note: Found + fixed an AED 456,697 GL hourly-labour misposting bug, plus a truthiness fix. 3-way reconciliation delta = 0.00 exact across 9,185 BOMs. Guarded the engineering backfill's BOM recompute hazard (137,046 cost estimates).

title: Root-cause audit: acme->vams schema-rename load-completeness gaps
note: All 0-row/1-row table gaps traced to one mechanism: service DBs originally loaded ETL into an 'acme' schema, later renamed wholesale to 'vams' by deploy_vams_tenant.py. Any table/spec not executed during that original 'acme' run sat empty in 'vams'. Fix everywhere = re-running the existing (already-correct) loaders with ETL_TARGET_SCHEMA=vams -- these were load-completeness gaps, not missing functionality.
